title: Stop fearing fructose from fruit and honey

chunks.title: The Benefits of Fruit
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the advantages of consuming fruit, emphasizing its natural sweetness and nutritional value. He highlights the Hadza tribe's preference for fruit and honey, illustrating that these foods do not lead to insulin resistance.
chunks.transcript: why am i a fan of fruit because it's the sweet part of the plant it's the part that the plant actually wants you to eat it's colorful it's clear the plant wants you to eat this and that humans always seek this the hadza always seek fruit when it's available they'll always eat honey when it's available if you guys follow my buddy liver king brian johnson you saw that he was recently in hot and you saw that he was recently in tanzania with the hadza same tribe that i visited last year and he was sticking his hand into a tree to get honey and then they were all just celebrating the fact they were eating this honey the hottest don't fear honey it doesn't make them insulin resistant didn't make me insulin resistant it won't make you insulin

chunks.title: Fructose Metabolism
chunks.summary: This segment delves into the metabolism of fructose, specifically how it is processed in the liver versus the small intestine. He addresses concerns about fructose's potential to cause fatty liver and discusses its metabolic pathways.

chunks.title: Fructose Clearance Mechanism
chunks.summary: The speaker explains how the small intestine can clear fructose and the importance of prior exposure to fructose in enhancing this clearance. He also contrasts the health effects of fiber-rich fruits with processed sugars.
chunks.transcript: liver where they cause toxicity liver or intestine specific khk keto hexokinase knockout mice would be a fascinating experiment now they'll go on to talk about the fact that intestinal fructose clearance is enhanced by both prior fructose exposure and by feeding so if you're eating fruit your body can upregulate the enzymatic systems in the small intestine to really process this now they say here a key difference between the health effects of fiber rich fruits perhaps even solid sweets like cake i would not agree with that and juices sodas is the rate of intestinal fructose release so i think that what we're seeing here is perhaps a mechanism of evolutionarily consistent consumption of fructose in fruit with all the other

chunks.title: Fiber and Fructose Interaction
chunks.summary: In this segment, the relationship between fiber and fructose is discussed, particularly how fiber may help slow down fructose absorption, making it less harmful. The speaker shares his perspective on fiber intake, suggesting that moderate amounts of fruit are beneficial.
chunks.transcript: precursors etc honey with nitric oxide precursors and here we go you guys are going to love this maybe this is a benefit of fiber if you're going to eat fructose you might as well eat it and fruit with a little bit of fiber maybe that slows down the absorption allows the small intestine to do it more in general if you guys have followed me for any amount of time you know that i'm not a fiber snob i'm not a fiber zealot i don't believe that fiber is the end-all be-all for a variety of reasons if you're getting some fiber and fruit i don't think that's horrible for you but i'm not one of these guys that believes you need more fiber more fiber more fiber people sometimes ask me how do you get enough fiber without vegetables and i say i don't think you need more fiber than fluorescent fruit honestly i actually think there's a lot of good evidence that humans don't really need much fiber at all but in the case of fructose fiber may be beneficial maybe the fruit and fiber is somehow playing into this role in the small intestine regardless

chunks.title: Fructose Consumption Guidelines
chunks.summary: The speaker presents guidelines for fructose consumption, suggesting that moderate amounts can be safely included in a diet. He backs his claims with research, giving an example of safe fructose limits for individuals on an animal-based diet.
